title: Rubus neofuscifolius
intro: Rubus neofuscifolius is a species of bramble or thorny shrub native to East Asia, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Rubus neofuscifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various respiratory and digestive issues.

It is further classified within the genus Rubus, which includes blackberries and raspberries. This species is native to Taiwan and is a member of the Rubus sect. Coreanicae. The classification of Rubus neofuscifolius is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and arrangement.
